mission.title: Install and Configure Kind (Kubernetes in Docker)
mission.description: Kind (Kubernetes in Docker) is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container nodes. It is primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself but can also be used for local development or CI. Key trade-offs include the need for Docker and the limitation of running in a single-node or multi-node configuration without the complexity of a full cloud setup.

steps.title: Install Kind
steps.description:
This step installs Kind using the stable binary for your platform. Choose the values that match your machine: `<your-os>` is `linux` or `darwin`, and `<your-arch>` is `amd64` or `arm64`.

```bash

curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-<your-os>-<your-arch>

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> Replace `<your-os>` with `linux` or `darwin`.
> Replace `<your-arch>` with `amd64` or `arm64`.
> Make the Kind binary executable.
> Move Kind to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kind Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a local Kubernetes cluster using Kind.

```bash

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> Creates a cluster with Kubernetes version 1.31.0

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step writes the Kind cluster connection details into your kubeconfig.

```bash

kind export kubeconfig --name kind

```

> Export the kubeconfig for the default Kind cluster named `kind`.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the cluster nodes are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the status of the nodes in the cluster

uninstall.title: Remove Kind Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kind cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all workloads and data in the cluster.

```bash

kind delete cluster

```

> Delete the Kind cluster

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kind
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup any important data. Download the release that matches your platform, then replace the existing binary.

```bash

curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-<your-os>-<your-arch>

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> Replace `<your-os>` with `linux` or `darwin`.
> Replace `<your-arch>` with `amd64` or `arm64`.
> Make the new Kind binary executable.
> Replace the old Kind binary with the new one.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kind: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind binary is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the directory containing the Kind binary is added to your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to start docker container'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker is not running or not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Start Docker and ensure it is running correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubelet service failed to start in the Kind cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Kind cluster and restart the cluster if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Kubernetes version image is not available.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are using a valid Kubernetes version and that your Docker daemon can access the internet.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved error messages for cluster creation failures.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: When everything is working, you should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing the nodes in the Kind cluster with a status of 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running on your machine.
